notes: Light-touch KB Tuesday. Ran the prescribed plan, no surprises. **TGU pattern: going 2 forward + 2 reverse per side** — adding the reverse direction (standing → lying) on top of the standard forward direction. Worth baking into the W12 TGU note. User asked for new flows/skills from the repo to rotate in — see W11+ planning notes for candidates.

sets.notes: New pattern: 2 reps in the standard direction, then 2 reps in reverse (standing → lying). Adds a skill dimension on top of the prescribed 2-per-side count.

notes: Shoulder feels better this week. **Equipment used:** prescribed setup for everything except lat pulldown — face pull, high cable curl, cable crunch all on the prescribed cable column. Lat pulldown forced to dual pulley (regular lat pulldown N/A) — user calibration: 'it's like half the weight, so 70 is 140 more or less.' **Other forced subs from availability (NOT user preference):** chest-supported row 30 (35 N/A), DB reverse fly 20 (22.5 N/A — 'none of the fucking weights are around'). **Curl exercise correction:** the bicep work is `incline-curl` (back-against-incline-bench DB curl, 2s pause at bottom), NOT `concentration-curl`. User direct quote: 'I have literally never done these [concentration curls] and if you took notes you would have known.' Per user history: 'it used to be preacher curl and i started an incline curl bc the preacher curl is always taken and then you just drifted.' Prior W7/W8/W10 entries that used `concentration-curl` were mislabeled — fixed those too. High cable curl on cable column 150x12 each side x2 (one arm at a time, could go up — first cable-column data point for this lift). Cable crunch cable column 120 a bit easy. **User meta-feedback on workout authoring:** stop putting notes-to-Claude inside the user-facing workout text (e.g., the W11 'log this fact every session' note about the lat pulldown 5lb increment was internal context, not for the user).

notes: W11D5 — solid legs day, several upward moves from prescribed loads. Squat: couldn't find 10lb plates, so jumped from prescribed 65 to **70/side x 8 x 4** — felt 'pretty easy and good for all sets'; user thinks 'QL finally caught up or at least getting there.' Leg press: went **slightly deeper than medium ROM**, all sets pretty easy, good stretch — user could feel where the QL are 'stronger in the middle.' Calf raises: chose **Option A (double-leg slow)** as recommended, slightly easy, all sets done. Leg curls: ramped past the prescribed 50 hard-reset — 50x12 too easy, 65x12 eccentrics easy, then 90x12 (got too hard around the 4th eccentric, finished last set) and another 90x12 — **90 is the working weight at corrected ankle placement** (per user: 'position wise looks good'). User flagged that **'this new order makes leg curls really easy for some reason' even though doing them correctly** (W11 moved leg curl earlier — leg press → calf raise → leg curl, vs W10 which had back ext + 2 lifts before leg curl). **Naming correction:** user flagged the prescription's 'back extension' label as **WRONG — this is incline RDL**, not back extensions. Workout JSON updated to use `incline-rdl` going forward. Lift itself: 25lb plate, super slow, all sets done, rested more between sets, squeezed glutes. Lunges not in user's input — likely skipped or not logged.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Couldn't find 10lb plates — went to 70/side instead of prescribed 65.
All 4 sets — 'pretty easy and good for all sets'. User: 'i think my QL finally caught up or at least getting there.'

sets.weight: 3 plates/side, slightly deeper than medium ROM
sets.notes: Felt really good.
Pretty easy all sets. Good stretch — user: 'can feel where the QL are stronger in the middle.' Went slightly past the prescribed medium ROM with no QL flag.
Slow tempo as recommended.
Difficulty was ok / slightly easy, did all sets. Option A (double-leg slow) was the right call — much more sustainable than W10D5's single-leg + slow combo.
Too easy — ramped up.
Eccentrics also easy.
Did four eccentrics and then got too hard, finished the last set. Probably working weight.
Another set — somewhat working weight. User: 'checked everything looks good position wise.' This is the corrected-lever 90, not the wrong-lever 90/100 from W7-W10.

exercises.notes: User: 'this new order makes leg curls really easy for some reason even though I'm doing them correctly.' W11 ordering put leg curl after leg press + calf raise (vs W10 which had back ext + leg press + calf raise + leg curl). Working weight at corrected ankle placement is settling around **90**.

sets.notes: Felt good.
Squeezed glutes, rested more between sets.
All sets done.

exercises.notes: **Naming correction (user flagged):** 'MARKED WRONG not back extensions how did this drift so hard.' This is the **incline RDL** on the 45° bench — hip hinge, not a back extension. Created `exercises/incline-rdl.json` and updated W11 legs workout to use it. **Audit completed W11D5:** past workouts (W7/W8/W10 legs) and past logs (W7/W8/W10) have been retroactively corrected to use `incline-rdl`. W1-W6 stay as `barbell-romanian-deadlift` (those were the actual barbell RDL on the squat rack — different lift).
